text:
一椀Mantra Siddhi Sādhana Manual
Introduction
This is the sacred manual for the practice of Nimbū-Mantra Siddhi. The lemon is used as a living yantra,
consecrated with mantra and infused with akti for protection, banishing, stambhana (stilling), or ākaraa
(attraction). Follow the instructions with discipline, purity, and dharmic intent.
1. Bhūta-uddhi (Elemental Purification)
• Sit on kua grass or clean cloth. Sprinkle water with mantra: 倀〉`〉
〉┉儿`〉 ℉〉
儿儿〉``
• Perform 9 prāāyāma breaths, purifying Earth, Water, Fire, Air, Ether.
• Nyāsa: Head ‒ 倀 Throat ‒ 倀儿 Heart ‒ 倀〉 Navel ‒ 倀 Feet ‒ 倀
• Invoke deity: 〉〉 〉
• Seal with kavacha: 倀 〉〉一3×).
2. Lemon Yantra Preparation
• Take fresh, untouched lemon.
• Inscribe yantra according to purpose:
- Uccāana: Circle + X + 4 dots
- Stambhana: 愀椀爀攀椀
- Ākaraa: Downward triangle + bindu
3. Mantra Japa & Infusion
• Uccāana (Banishing): 倀〉儿 `† 108× for 7 days, face
South.
• Stambhana (Stilling): 倀儿…倀† 108× for
11 nights, face NE.
• Ākaraa (Attraction): 倀`儿儿 `† 108× for 21 days,
face East.
• Always close with: 倀``
4. Disposal (Visarjana)
• Uccāana: 7th day → discard in river or cremation ground, don’t look back.
• Stambhana: 11th day → bury under tree or

th: 倀``
4. Disposal (Visarjana)
• Uccāana: 7th day → discard in river or cremation ground, don’t look back.
• Stambhana: 11th day → bury under tree or at crossroads.
• Ākaraa: 21st day → immerse in flowing water, offer 5 red flowers + sweets.
• After disposal, bathe and recite Hanumān Cālīsā or Viu Sahasranāma.
5. Rules of the Cycle
• Only one yantra at a time.
• If a day is missed, restart with fresh lemon.
• Never eat or keep consecrated lemons.
• Maintain discipline and dharmic intent.
Closing

On the final day, sit silently, chant 11×:
倀〉〉
(May all this be offered into Brahman).
